code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multiple defects overriding initial satisfaction
    - Fundamental usability failures despite interesting concept
    - Negative updates demonstrating worsening performance
    - Explicit warnings against purchase with defect evidence

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires constant workarounds
    - "Better alternatives exist" comparisons with justification
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Regret expressed through cost/value complaints

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...") without endorsement
    - Technical adequacy with clear quality compromises
    - Target-specific utility without enthusiasm

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Brief factual positivity without enthusiasm ("does the job")
    - Single functional flaw impacting core experience
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5")
    - Resolved issues mentioned as lingering drawbacks
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - 3+ praised aspects (functionality/durability/design) despite single flaw
    - Close comparisons to premium brands with satisfied performance
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core functional praise
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Concise praise ("Perfect fit!") without negatives
    - Long-term validation overcoming initial skepticism
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" endorsements with minor imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Functional praise + single functional flaw →4 unless 3+ aspects praised
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if satisfaction expressed
    - Core function excellence post-setup →5 ("worth the effort")
    - Descriptive positivity + satisfaction markers →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster than first. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Not quite Lodge but very close! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "This is a sharp well made tablecloth. Strengths: vibrant colors, sharp contrast, solid construction. Only con: needs ironing."
<Reasoning>3+ functional strengths override non-functional criticism →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Works after washing but edges stay stiff."
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Between puppy/kid messes, this cleaner surprised me with effectiveness!"
<Reasoning>Exceeded expectations solving persistent problem →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Constant adjustments needed for basic function."
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Good for guest room but too flimsy for daily use."
<Reasoning>Contextual limitation with compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Assembly took hours but finally works adequately."
<Reasoning>Significant effort for basic functionality →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "While comfort is 4 stars, durability and design make it 5-star overall"
<Reasoning>Multiple strengths outweigh single criticism →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>

  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 5 -> 4: 3
  gold 1 -> 5: 1
  gold 2 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):

==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 1, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 24 errors, 26 borderline out of 80 examples."}
